content.problemStatement:
Chef really admires Gordon Ramsay. Both of them have restaurant chains. While Chef's restaurant chain consists of NNN facilities (numbered 111 through NNN), Gordon Ramsay has only 333, and yet Gordon's restaurants are superior. Chef's marketing team has figured out how to take over the market. According to them, the problem is that the customers have too many restaurants to choose from and that Gordon's staff, by accident or by sheer marketing genius, has figured out the perfect distribution of restaurants. Therefore, Chef wants to close down N‚àí3N-3N‚àí3 restaurants as fast as possible and create a restaurant structure similar to Gordon Ramsay's. For that, he needs your help!

We know that Gordon Ramsay's restaurants are numbered 1,2,31, 2, 31,2,3, where restaurant 111 is the smallest and restaurant 333 is the largest. They are located on a line in the order p1,p2,p3p_1, p_2, p_3p1‚Äã,p2‚Äã,p3‚Äã, so the restaurants p1p_1p1‚Äã and p2p_2p2‚Äã are adjacent and the restaurants p2p_2p2‚Äã and p3p_3p3‚Äã are adjacent too.

Chef's restaurant system is a tree with NNN vertices, where each vertex is one of his precious restaurants. Just like with Gordon's restaurants, they are numbered in the order of increasing size. Chef wants to count the number of ordered triples of restaurants (a1,a2,a3)(a_1, a_2, a_3)(a1‚Äã,a2‚Äã,a3‚Äã) which he can keep open while satisfying the following rules:


The restaurants a1a_1a1‚Äã, a2a_2a2‚Äã and a3a_3a3‚Äã are located on a line, i.e. a2a_2a2‚Äã is on the shortest path between a1a_1a1‚Äã and a3a_3a3‚Äã (rebuilding the existing structure would be too costly).
For each i,ji, ji,j (1‚â§i,j‚â§31 \le i, j \le 31‚â§i,j‚â§3), if pi<pjp_i \lt p_jpi‚Äã<pj‚Äã, then ai<aja_i \lt a_jai‚Äã<aj‚Äã.
Help Chef and calculate the number of these triples!
constraints: the graph of Chef's restaurants is a tree
the sequence (p1,p2,p3)(p_1, p_2, p_3)(p1‚Äã,p2‚Äã,p3‚Äã) is a permutation of (1,2,3)(1, 2, 3)(1,2,3)
**Subtask #3 (10 points):** the tree is a star graph
**Subtask #4 (15 points):** the tree is a path graph
**Subtask #5 (30 points):** the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains three space-separated integers p1p_1p1‚Äã, p2p_2p2‚Äã and p3p_3p3‚Äã.
Each of the next N‚àí1N-1N‚àí1 lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv denoting that the restaurants uuu and vvv are connected by an edge.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äï the number of triples.

Explanation:
Example case 1: The only valid triple is (3,2,4)(3, 2, 4)(3,2,4).
